author: Carlos Dias
text:
Antimicrobial Stewardship (AMS) is no longer only about producing evidence, developing guidelines or defining policy priorities.

The real challenge is implementation.

Across Europe, healthcare systems are working to translate antimicrobial stewardship principles into measurable improvements in clinical practice, governance, professional competencies and patient outcomes.

As Portugal Liaison Officer for EU-JAMRAI II, I have the opportunity to contribute to this effort by supporting collaboration between European and national stakeholders, facilitating knowledge exchange, promoting implementation of best practices and strengthening alignment between European priorities and national action plans.

Through the One Health approach, antimicrobial resistance is addressed as a shared challenge across human health, animal health and the environment.

Preserving the effectiveness of antimicrobials requires more than awareness. It requires coordination, engagement, implementation and continuous learning.

Because implementation is where antimicrobial stewardship creates real impact.

#EUJAMRAI2 #AntimicrobialStewardship #AMR #OneHealth #PublicHealth #HealthcareLeadership #ImplementationScience #HealthPolicy #PPCIRA #DGS

author: Pedro Herrera
text:
Hay métricas que se reportan.

Y métricas que realmente importan.

Ingresos, crecimiento, eficiencia…
todo eso es necesario.

Pero hay un indicador que no aparece en ningún dashboard,
y que, sin embargo, lo condiciona todo:

Que la gente quiera volver a trabajar contigo.
Porque eso no se consigue con autoridad.

Ni con incentivos puntuales.
Ni con discursos bien construidos.

Se consigue con experiencia.

Con cómo haces sentir a las personas en el día a día.
Con cómo gestionas los momentos difíciles.
Con el respeto, la coherencia y la confianza que generas.

Ahí es donde se construye el liderazgo real.

Porque cuando alguien quiere volver a tu equipo,
no está eligiendo un proyecto.

Te está eligiendo a ti.

📌 Puedes forzar resultados a corto plazo.
📌 Pero la lealtad solo se gana a largo plazo.

Y esa lealtad es lo que convierte a un equipo en algo mucho más sólido que una estructura.

Lo convierte en un proyecto compartido.

#Liderazgo #Equipos #CulturaEmpresarial #Talento #Management #Confianza

author: LCR Capital Partners
text:
For families evaluating EB-5, immigration eligibility and securities eligibility are two separate—but equally important—parts of the process.

Because most EB-5 offerings are structured as private securities transactions, investors may need to qualify as US accredited investors, or participate through a Regulation S offshore framework. Understanding which pathway applies—and what documentation is required—is a critical step before subscribing to any offering.

Our latest article explains how to qualify, the difference between Reg D and Reg S investors, and what EB-5 families should be prepared to provide.

Read the full article here: https://hubs.li/Q04lsrtb0

author: The Water Entrepreneur Podcast
text:
How does a growing city manage more than 600 miles of water infrastructure while keeping water loss low and planning for the future?

In our latest interview at the city of Santa Fe Water Division, City Engineer Taylor Jurgens discusses the challenges of managing a diverse water system that serves up to 90,000 residents and relies on a mix of surface water and groundwater sources.

🏔️ From mountain snowpack and Rio Grande diversions to aging pipes dating back to the 1800s, Santa Fe's water system presents unique opportunities and challenges for utility managers.

Taylor also shares how ASTERRA's satellite leak detection data can help utilities focus resources, identify hidden leaks, support asset management efforts, and make smarter infrastructure investment decisions.

Watch the full interview to learn how Santa Fe is proactively managing its water system and preparing for future growth.

#WaterInfrastructure #LeakDetection #WaterConservation #AssetManagement #ASTERRA #SantaFe #WaterUtilities #NonRevenueWater #InfrastructureInnovation #WaterManagement

author: Theresia K.
text:
I spent 20 years optimizing for the wrong thing.
Output. Productivity. How much I could get done in a day.

I thought that was the game.
It wasn't.

The professionals I talk to now who are actually building wealth aren't asking “how do I make more?”
They're asking a sharper question:

“How do I protect my time while my money keeps working?”

That question changes everything.
It changes which deals make sense. Which opportunities are worth a phone call. Which ones, no matter how good the IRR looks, require more of you than you're willing to give.

Because here's the truth I had to learn the hard way:
A 20% return that costs you your weekends isn't a 20% return.

It's a job with extra steps.
The investments worth your time are the ones that don't ask for any.

If you had to choose between doubling your income or doubling your time, which one would you actually pick?

author: Mukul Soni
text:
One question from a recent leadership discussion stayed with me.

After presenting a transformation concept, the conversation quickly shifted away from technology.

The questions were simple:
• How will it increase revenue?
• How will it reduce operating costs?
• How will it reduce infrastructure costs?

Not a single question was about AI models, platforms, or technical architecture.
And that's exactly how executive discussions should happen.

Most leaders are not looking for more technology.
They are looking for measurable business outcomes.

Over the years, I've seen many transformation initiatives begin with discussions about systems and features.

The more successful ones begin with different questions:
Where is value being lost?
Which costs can be optimized?
What decisions can be improved?
How quickly can results be realized?

Technology matters.
But technology is rarely the destination.

It is the enabler that helps organizations improve visibility, make better decisions, and achieve better outcomes.

The strongest business case for AI is not that it is intelligent.
The strongest business case is that it helps organizations create more value from the resources they already have.

That is where transformation becomes meaningful.

#AI #BusinessTransformation #Leadership #OperationalExcellence #DigitalTransformation #BusinessStrategy #RevenueGrowth

author: Sandeep Chandran
text:
Most companies think they have a go-to-market problem.

They don't. They have a market problem.

They launch campaigns before defining:
 • The market they're trying to win
 • The buyers they need to reach
 • The problems they solve
 • Why they're different from competitors

Then they wonder why growth stalls. More leads won't fix the wrong market.

More SDRs won't fix weak positioning. More AI won't fix a strategy gap.

High-growth companies don't start with campaigns.

They start with market definition, customer understanding, competitive positioning, and a clear execution path.

Execution scales strategy. It doesn't replace it.

Read our latest insight: https://lnkd.in/g8qzBxN9

#GoToMarket #GTMStrategy #B2BMarketing #RevenueGrowth #ProductMarketing #MarketingStrategy #SalesStrategy #BusinessGrowth #CompetitivePositioning #ElevateGTMSolutions

text:
Excited to share my project, based on Python, under the internship of Oasis Infobyte (python programming Internship) 🚀✨

     This project is based on the basic Client–Server communication model where data is exchanged between a client program and a server program. The server continuously waits for requests, and once the client connects, both can send and receive messages.
     This simple implementation helped me understand how networks work, how sockets are created, how connections are established, and how real-world applications communicate over networks.

This was a beginner friendly networking project and I want to say a Warm Thank you to Oasis Infobyte for providing this type of opportunities where learning and experiencing grows simultaneously ✨🎉

La elección del ingrediente adecuado influye directamente en la estandarización de procesos, el rendimiento, la inocuidad y la estabilidad de cada elaboración.
En Alimentari contamos con una línea de soluciones desarrolladas para la producción gastronómica e industrial:

✔ Albúmina en polvo, diseñada para aplicaciones donde el volumen, la estabilidad y el batido marcan la diferencia, como merengues, repostería y formulaciones especiales.
✔ Yema en polvo, una excelente alternativa para mayonesas, salsas, cremas y emulsiones de uso industrial.
Con stock local, productos 100% pasteurizados y más de una década apoyando a empresas del sector alimentario, seguimos trabajando para que cada proceso sea más eficiente y predecible.

author: Muhammad Mushtaq Hussain
text:
Septic System: How It Works

A septic system treats household wastewater underground through three stages:

1. Septic Tank – Wastewater enters the tank. Heavy solids settle at the bottom as sludge, while oils and grease float on top as scum.

2. Anaerobic Filter – Partially treated water passes through a filter filled with stones or media where bacteria break down remaining organic matter.

3. Absorption Field (Soak Pit) – The treated water slowly infiltrates the surrounding soil, where it is naturally filtered before reaching groundwater.

✅ Purpose: Safely treat wastewater, protect public health, and reduce environmental pollution.

#SepticSystem #SepticTank #WastewaterTreatment #CivilEngineering #EnvironmentalEngineering #Sanitation #WaterManagement #DrainageSystem #ConstructionEngineering #Infrastructure #GroundwaterProtection #SoakPit #AnaerobicFilter #EngineeringEducation

author: Clint Delos Santos
text:
Stop selling your expertise for pocket change. 🛑 Freelancers, we need to talk.

I’m seeing a deeply frustrating trend lately, and it needs to be addressed. Far too many Filipino Virtual Assistants are accepting $1 to $3 an hour.

I get it—money is money, and we all have bills to pay. But here is the hard truth: When you accept those rates, you aren't just underpaying yourself; you are lowering the bar for the entire Filipino freelancing community.

Because of this, foreign clients are starting to see $2/hr as our "standard" benchmark. They come here expecting premium, high-level skills for less than the price of a cup of coffee. Let’s call it what it is: it’s a form of digital exploitation. Foreigners are taking advantage of Filipino talent because they know people are desperate enough to take it.
We need to value our skills and experience. Stop letting the allure of a few dollars make you sell your years of expertise for less than you are worth. You are a professional, not cheap labor.

As the Founder and CEO of 21st VA, I built this team on a simple principle: dignity in talent. I refuse to let our team—or our community—be treated as a discount workforce. We are not a digital sweatshop.

At 21st VA, we refuse to participate in this race to the bottom. We don't just offer "services" or hand over task-takers—we offer strategic partnerships.

We don't bargain-basement our packages.
We aim to match our rates to the equivalent of a US daily wager, because the value we bring directly impacts their bottom line.

If we deliver US-level results, we deserve a dignified, livable wage.

If we want clients to respect us, we have to respect ourselves first. Say NO to lowball offers. Stand your ground. Know your worth, raise your rates, and let's put an end to cheap labor. 🇵🇭✨

#FreelancePh #VirtualAssistantPh #KnowYourWorth #StopCheapLabor #21stVA #FilipinoFreelancers #VACommunity #CEOInsights #Leadership
… más
